title: 8 Herbal Teas For Dry Cough
intro_desc: If you're struggling with a dry cough, herbal teas can be a great help. They work by soothing and calming your throat, reducing inflammation, and loosening mucus. This allows you to breathe easier and feel more comfortable. One of the main reasons herbal teas are effective is because they contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help to heal and protect your body.  For example, Thymus vulgaris, also known as thyme, is a popular herb for dry coughs. It contains compounds like thymol, which have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. When you drink thyme tea, it helps to kill the bacteria that can cause infections and reduce inflammation in your throat.  Another herb that's great for dry coughs is Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice root. Licorice root has anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and calm your throat, making it easier to swallow and breathe. It's also rich in antioxidants, which can help to protect your body from damage caused by free radicals.  Echinacea purpurea, also known as coneflower, is another herb that's often used to relieve dry coughs. It has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and pain in your throat, making it easier to breathe and swallow. Echinacea also has immunomodulatory effects, which means it can help to boost your immune system and prevent infections.  When you drink herbal teas like these, you can experience a range of benefits. You'll feel more comfortable and able to breathe easier, which can make a big difference in your daily life. You'll also be able to sleep better, as a dry cough can disrupt your sleep patterns. And, by reducing inflammation and killing bacteria, you'll be less likely to develop complications like pneumonia.
faq_1: Drinking herbal tea may help soothe a dry cough. Some herbal teas, like thyme and honey, have natural anti-inflammatory properties that can calm the throat. They can also provide moisture to the air, making it easier to cough up mucus. This can help ease a dry cough, especially when you're feeling under the weather.
faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help soothe a dry cough. Certain herbs like licorice root and slippery elm have anti-inflammatory properties that can calm the throat and reduce irritation. Herbal teas like ginger and peppermint can also ease congestion and loosen mucus, making it easier to cough up. This may provide some relief from a dry cough.
faq_safe: Herbal teas can be a soothing way to help with dry coughs. However, drinking them every day can be a problem. Some herbs, like licorice root, can have too much of a good thing, causing blood pressure to rise or interfering with medications. Moderation is key.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas can help soothe a dry cough, but it may take some time to feel the effects. Results can vary, but some people notice relief within a few days to a week after drinking the tea regularly. It's best to try different types to see what works for you.
faq_time: For a dry cough, it's best to drink herbal tea at night before bed. This helps your body relax and recover while you sleep, which is especially important when you're not feeling well. Your body can focus on healing without being distracted by daily activities.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with prescription medications for dry cough. Some herbal teas, like thyme and eucalyptus, have compounds that might increase the effects of certain medications, such as blood thinners. This could lead to complications, like bleeding or bruising. It's essential to be cautious when combining herbal teas with prescription medications.
faq_pregnant: If you're pregnant and have a dry cough, you might want to try herbal teas for some relief. However, it's best to be cautious with what you drink. Some herbal teas, like slippery elm and peppermint, are considered safe during pregnancy, but others, like echinacea and sage, might not be. Always check the ingredients carefully.
faq_breastfeeding: Herbal teas for dry cough can be a good option while breastfeeding. However, it's essential to check the ingredients and choose teas that are safe for both you and your baby. Some herbs like peppermint and ginger are commonly used and considered safe, but it's crucial to avoid teas with strong medications or stimulants that could pass into breast milk.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris teas contains thymol and carvacrol, two potent active constituents. These compounds have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe a dry cough by reducing the inflammation in the throat and airways. Thymol's expectorant properties also help to loosen and clear mucus, making it easier to cough up and relieve congestion. Carvacrol's antiseptic properties help to combat any underlying infections that may be contributing to the cough. By reducing inflammation and fighting off infections, Thymus vulgaris teas can help to alleviate the discomfort of a dry cough.
remedies.image_alt: Thymus vulgaris herbal teas for dry cough
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of boiling water and 1 tablespoon of dried Thymus vulgaris leaves.
Measure 1 teaspoon of dried Thymus vulgaris leaves into a tea infuser or a small piece of cheesecloth.
Steep the Thymus vulgaris leaves in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the Thymus vulgaris leaves.
Drink the Thymus vulgaris tea while it is still warm to help soothe a dry cough.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas contains glycyrrhizin, a triterpenoid saponin, and flavonoids like isoliquertin and licoricidin. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and calm the throat, reducing irritation and discomfort associated with dry cough. Glycyrrhizin also has expectorant properties, which help to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to cough up. Additionally, flavonoids in Glycyrrhiza glabra teas have antioxidant properties that protect the throat and airways from oxidative stress, further reducing inflammation and discomfort. By reducing inflammation and loosening mucus, Glycyrrhiza glabra teas can provide relief from dry cough symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal teas for dry cough
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 tablespoon of dried Glycyrrhiza glabra root powder and 1 cup of water.
Boil the water in a pot and let it cool for 1-2 minutes.
Add the Glycyrrhiza glabra root powder to the water and stir well.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes, then strain it into a cup.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help relieve dry cough symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea teas contains alkylamides, caffeic acid, and polysaccharides as its bioactive constituents. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and calm the irritated throat and airways associated with a dry cough. The alkylamides in Echinacea purpurea teas have been shown to increase the production of mucus, which can help to trap and clear out irritants and bacteria that may be contributing to the cough. The caffeic acid in Echinacea purpurea teas has antioxidant properties that can help to reduce inflammation and fight off infections that may be causing the dry cough. By reducing inflammation and increasing mucus production, Echinacea purpurea teas can help to provide relief from a dry cough.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal teas for dry cough
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Echinacea purpurea roots and flowers.
Combine the Echinacea roots and flowers with 1 cup of boiling water in a heat-resistant cup.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes, then strain it using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Add honey to taste, if needed, to make the tea sweeter.
Drink the Echinacea tea 2-3 times a day for relief from dry cough.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale teas contains compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. These compounds help reduce inflammation in the throat and airways, making it easier to cough up mucus. Gingerols and shogaols also have a soothing effect on the throat and lungs, helping to calm a dry cough. Additionally, Zingiber officinale teas has demulcent properties, which help create a protective barrier on the throat and airways, further reducing irritation and discomfort. By reducing inflammation and creating a soothing barrier, Zingiber officinale teas helps alleviate the discomfort of a dry cough.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal teas for dry cough
remedy_procedure: Get 1 cup of water and boil it. Turn off the heat.
Measure 1 tablespoon of dried Zingiber officinale root. Crush it finely.
Add the crushed Zingiber officinale root to the boiled water. Let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth. Discard the solids.
Drink the tea while it's warm. You can add honey to taste, if needed.

remedy_amazon.title: FGO Organic Ginger Tea, 100 Count, Eco-Conscious Tea Bags, Caffeine Free, Packaging May Vary (Pack of 1)

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus teas contains compounds like cineole, globulol, and alpha-pinene, which are known to have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. These properties help break down mucus in the airways, making it easier to cough up. The cineole in eucalyptus globulus teas also has a decongestant effect, which helps relieve congestion and open up airways. The anti-inflammatory properties of the globulol and alpha-pinene help reduce swelling in the throat and airways, further relieving dry cough symptoms. By combining these effects, eucalyptus globulus teas helps provide quick relief from dry cough.
remedies.image_alt: Eucalyptus globulus herbal teas for dry cough
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Eucalyptus globulus leaves.
Use a tea infuser or a saucepan to steep 1 tablespoon of leaves in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using the tea infuser or a fine-mesh sieve.
Add honey or lemon to taste, if needed, to soothe the throat and calm the cough.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help relieve dry cough symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Sambucus nigra teas contains bioactive constituents like flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids that have anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties. These compounds help reduce inflammation in the throat and airways, making it easier to breathe and swallow. The flavonoids, especially rutin and quercetin, have antioxidant properties that fight free radicals and protect the body from oxidative stress. The terpenoids, including limonene and beta-pinene, have antitussive (cough-suppressing) effects that help alleviate dry cough symptoms. By soothing the throat and reducing inflammation, Sambucus nigra teas can provide quick relief for dry cough.
remedies.image_alt: Sambucus nigra herbal teas for dry cough
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Sambucus nigra flowers. If using fresh, dry them in a low oven (150°F - 200°F) for 2 hours.
Measure 1 tablespoon of Sambucus nigra flowers and place them in a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the flowers. If using a tea infuser, make sure it's fully submerged in the water.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes. Strain the tea into another cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Drink the tea hot or warm, up to 3 times a day, to help relieve a dry cough.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita teas contains several bioactive constituents, including menthol, menthone, and limonene, which contribute to its effectiveness in treating dry cough. Menthol, in particular, has a cooling effect on the throat, helping to relieve irritation and discomfort. The antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties of menthone also help to soothe the throat and reduce inflammation, making it easier to cough up mucus. The expectorant properties of limonene help to thin and loosen mucus, making it easier to expel and relieving congestion. By combining these properties, Mentha x piperita teas helps to provide quick relief from dry cough symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Mentha x piperita herbal teas for dry cough
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Mentha x piperita leaves or 1 tablespoon of dried leaves.
Boil 1 cup of water in a pot and let it cool for 1 minute.
Add the Mentha x piperita leaves to the cooled water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup and discard the leaves.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help relieve dry cough.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia teas contains compounds like linalool and linalyl acetate, which have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. These properties help to calm the throat and reduce inflammation, making it a good remedy for dry cough. The antispasmodic effect of linalool and linalyl acetate also helps to relax the muscles in the throat, allowing for easier breathing. Additionally, the tea's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, providing relief from a dry cough. By reducing inflammation and loosening mucus, Lavandula angustifolia teas can provide relief from dry cough symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Lavandula angustifolia herbal teas for dry cough
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 tablespoon of dried Lavandula angustifolia flowers and 1 cup of boiling water.
Place the dried flowers in a cup and pour the boiling water over them.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes to allow the flavors to release.
Strain the tea into another cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the flowers.
Drink the tea warm or at room temperature to help soothe a dry cough.
